definition: An emerging bifurcation of global yield curves along the sanctions perimeter: allied-bloc and non-aligned-bloc curves cannot converge due to sanctions, capital controls, and compliance costs. The US-China 10Y spread (~220bp) is not arbitrageable for most institutions.
evidence: US-China 10Y spread (~220bp) is not arbitrageable for most institutions due to sanctions/compliance
Sanctions and capital controls create friction preventing cross-perimeter yield convergence
Novel structural observation not addressed by other agents
open_questions: Is this a transitional phase or a permanent regime change?
How does the two-tier structure affect global capital flows and reserve currency dynamics?
What is the welfare cost of the arbitrage barrier?
